apparatus.name: Volumetric Flask
apparatus.description: Used to prepare solutions of an accurate fixed volume. Its narrow neck helps ensure precise concentration during solution preparation.

apparatus.name: Funnel
apparatus.description: Used to transfer liquids or powders into containers without spilling. It also supports filtration when used with filter paper.

apparatus.name: EDTA Crystals
apparatus.description: EDTA crystals are used to prepare the standard titrant solution. They react with metal ions to determine water hardness.

apparatus.name: Beaker
apparatus.description: A general-purpose laboratory container used for mixing, heating, and holding liquids. It provides approximate volume measurements.

apparatus.name: Burette
apparatus.description: A graduated glass tube used to dispense liquids accurately during titration. It allows controlled delivery of the titrant drop by drop.

apparatus.name: Pipette
apparatus.description: Used to measure and transfer a fixed volume of liquid with high precision. Commonly used for collecting the sample solution.

apparatus.name: Electric Balance
apparatus.description: An electronic instrument used to measure the mass of chemicals accurately. It ensures precise weighing before solution preparation.

apparatus.name: Wash bottle
apparatus.description: A squeeze bottle used to dispense distilled water for rinsing glassware or adding small amounts of water during experiments.

apparatus.name: Weighing Bottle
apparatus.description: A small container used to safely hold and transfer solid chemicals during weighing. It helps prevent contamination and material loss.

substeps.task: Place the electronic balance on the table.
Place the weighing bottle on the balance.
Bring the EDTA crystals near the balance.
Transfer EDTA crystals into the weighing bottle.
Adjust the quantity until the balance reads 0.372 g.
Place the volumetric flask on the table.
Insert the funnel into the flask.
Pour the weighed EDTA crystals into the flask through the funnel.
Remove the funnel from the flask.
Bring the wash bottle near the flask.
Add distilled water until the flask is about half full.
Swirl the flask gently to dissolve the EDTA crystals.
Add distilled water up to the calibration mark.
Close the volumetric flask with its stopper.
Invert the flask several times to ensure uniform mixing.
